page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for single-core operations, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1150.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 8118.07. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by the stress-ng multi-core score of 5749.22 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4273.0. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching up to 414.69 GB/sec for small cached blocks, but large-block write bandwidth is weak at 23.56 GB/sec. The instance handles lightweight LLM inference on small models adequately but performs poorly on medium 7B models.

The ecs.cm4.2xlarge offers dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring consistent compute resources without noisy-neighbor interference. However, the underlying Broadwell-generation Intel Xeon E5 v4 architecture lacks modern instruction sets like AVX-512, limiting its efficiency in cryptography and advanced vector math. Given the balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and moderate network throughput, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for legacy general-purpose workloads, static web serving, Redis caching, and basic software compilation, rather than intensive database operations or modern machine learning workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, this KVM-based instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmarks reveal average single-core performance, software compilation, and Redis operations, but weak multi-core scaling and write memory bandwidth. The absence of AVX-512 instructions limits its cryptographic and vector processing efficiency. Consequently, the ecs.cm4.2xlarge is best suited for legacy general-purpose applications, static web serving, and lightweight caching workloads where dedicated CPU resources are required but cutting-edge compute performance is not critical.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for static web serving and Redis caching.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.2xlarge is a general-purpose KVM instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core x86 virtual server with balanced memory allocation for standard web serving and caching workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average performance in Redis operations and static web serving
Weak multi-core scaling and lack of local storage or GPU
Best suited for legacy general-purpose workloads and lightweight caching
